---
title: "My R Markdown Primer"
author: "Priscilla Guillen"
date: "2026-05-04"
output: html_document
---

## Introduction
R Markdown is a tool used to combine code, output, and text into one document. It 
allows users to create reports that are reproducible and easy to understand.

## Explanation
Using R Markdown makes it easier to organize analysis because everything is in one
place. Instead of writing code seperately, users can explain their process while 
showing results

## Math example
Inline equation $a + b = c$
Displayed equation:
$$
x = \frac{a + b}{2}
$$

```{r}
data("iris")
head(iris)

```

```{r}
summary(iris)
plot(iris$Petal.Length, iris$Petal.Width)
```

## Conclusion
Using R Markdown was helpful because it allows me to combine code and explanations
in one document. It makes analysis more clear and organized.
